overview: The orchestration layer turning per-section sentence queues into sequential TTS playback: AudioPlayerService (1242-line core, 197 commits of churn), PlaybackStateManager (queue/index/skip-masks), TaskSequencer (serial task chain), PlatformIntegration/MediaSessionManager/BackgroundAudio (lock-screen + keep-alive), TTSProviderManager (provider selection/fallback), TTSFlightRecorder (diagnostics), and the src/lib/tts/engine/ boundary (EngineContext/PlaybackBackend/AudioSink ports, WorkerEngineContext state replication, WorkerTtsEngine + createWorkerEngineClient Comlink bridge, WorkerEngineHandle façade). Production always runs the engine in a Web Worker (mainThreadAudioPlayer.ts:29-34); the in-process path survives for tests. State replicates main→worker via a declarative replicationSpec; engine writes return as host commands; audio/platform calls proxy back to the main thread.

debts.title: Provider-event and gesture paths bypass the TaskSequencer
debts.fix: Route every externally-triggered transition (fallback recovery, dragnet, provider events) through the sequencer; dev-mode assert that status/queue mutation only happens inside a running task.

debts.title: applySkippedMask mutates queue in place: broken dirty-check + transport-divergent UI
debts.severity: critical (overstated: Every cited mechanism is real (in-place mutation at PlaybackStateManager.ts:111 vs clone at 139/191, identity-based persistQueue no-op at 443, same-reference notify at 479-485, useShallow suppression )
debts.fix: Make PlaybackStateManager copy-on-write with immutable snapshots; add a parity scenario applying a skip mask and asserting fresh queue identity on both transports.

debts.title: AudioPlayerService god object (1242 lines, ~15 responsibilities)
debts.fix: Decompose into PlaybackController (FSM+sequencer), SessionRestoreService, MediaMetadataPublisher, AnalysisApplier, DragnetGesture, LexiconCache behind the existing ports; APS becomes a thin façade.

debts.title: Two notification paths emit inconsistent snapshots
debts.fix: Single immutable PlaybackSnapshot object with a monotonic sequence number, emitted from exactly one place.

debts.title: Replication echo loop: full store (incl. queue) re-serialized into the worker per sentence
debts.fix: Split useTTSStore into a settings store (replicated, data-only subset) and a playback mirror store (never replicated); broadcast queue only on queue-identity change or as deltas.

debts.title: Dual-transport contract unowned; parity suite covers a fraction of behavior
debts.fix: Define TtsEngine as a standalone interface both implementations satisfy, spec commands as acks, surface handle errors into the snapshot error channel, and grow parity scenarios to absorb the regression-test behaviors.

debts.title: Flight recorder split-brained across threads — diagnostics broken in production
debts.fix: Expose snapshot/exportBuffer on WorkerTtsEngine and route DiagnosticsTab through the engine handle.

debts.title: No cancellation in TaskSequencer; staleness via scattered hand-rolled guards
debts.fix: Epoch/AbortSignal-based cancellation in the sequencer with a ctx.checkStale() helper and per-task watchdog timeouts.

debts.title: Dead features on the hot path (SyncEngine no-op highlight, unused methods)
debts.fix: Delete SyncEngine + onMeta/onBoundary plumbing (or implement highlighting properly), remove dead members, type the mode parameter.

debts.title: Seek/position heuristics: seek-past-end rewinds to sentence 0; unthrottled native position pushes
debts.fix: Clamp seekToTime to the last visible index, factor speed into estimates, throttle position-state updates.

debts.title: TTSProviderManager: duplicated fallback, store/engine provider divergence, listener accumulation
debts.fix: Single fallback routine emitting a providerChanged engine event; capability interface for voice download; dispose listeners on swap.

debts.title: Test sprawl: 15 overlapping AudioPlayerService suites with copy-pasted mocks
debts.fix: Consolidate into behavior-grouped suites on FakeEngineContext/FakePlaybackBackend, promote durable behaviors into the shared parity scenarios, delete per-bug files.

debts.title: Boundary type leaks: TTSQueueItem in DB layer; settings snapshot types include stripped functions
debts.fix: Move TTSQueueItem/TTSStatus to src/types/tts.ts; define a data-only TTSSettingsData payload the store satisfies.

debts.title: BackgroundAudio dual staggered looping elements, undocumented, doubles noise amplitude
debts.fix: Document the keep-alive rationale; use a single seamless-looping source (or stagger only for silence).

debts.title: Documentation drift in engine READMEs
debts.fix: Regenerate both READMEs from current reality; retire PORTING-TO-WORKER.md's 'remaining step' framing.

couplings: src/types/db.ts:11 and src/db/DBService.ts:18 import TTSQueueItem from lib/tts/AudioPlayerService.ts — the persisted DB schema depends on the engine god file (reversed layering).

EngineContext.ts:26-60 derives boundary payload types from typeof useTTSStore/useGenAIStore/useReadingStateStore getState — engine contract structurally coupled to store implementations, including action functions that don't survive worker replication.
ReaderView.tsx (485, 903-905, 1011, 1297) and useTTS.ts:31-33 call getAudioPlayer() directly, including clearPauseGesture() — the reader UI must know the engine's dragnet gesture internals.

createWorkerEngineClient.ts duplicates createZustandEngineContext's host wiring (LexiconService, bookRepository, contentAnalysisRepository, genAIService, 6 Zustand stores) as a second composition root — applyHostCommand and the Zustand context can drift.

AudioPlayerService.ts:4 / PlaybackStateManager.ts:2 import dbService directly (deliberate, worker-safe) — persistence policy incl. the WebKit-hang workaround is baked into the orchestrator rather than behind a port.
DiagnosticsTab.tsx:22-59 reads the main-thread TTSFlightRecorder singleton while production recording happens in the worker's separate instance.
useTTSStore is bidirectionally coupled to the engine: proxies commands in, mirrors playback state out, and is replicated back into the worker as the 'settings' slice (echo loop).
keepers: The three-port engine boundary (EngineContext / PlaybackBackend / AudioSink) with explicit worker-safety rationale and excellent per-port documentation — build the refactor on it.

replicationSpec.ts declarative slice table with compile-time exhaustiveness, boot readiness gate (hasReplicated), and WorkerEngineContext's throw-on-unreplicated reads, all pinned by replication.test.ts.
Handwritten fakes (FakeEngineContext, FakePlaybackBackend, FakeAudioSink) enabling mock-free engine tests (AudioPlayerService.isolated.test.ts).
Shared engineParityScenarios run on both transports over real Comlink/MessageChannel — right mechanism, needs broader coverage.
WorkerEngineHandle boot-queue + whenReady()/engineReady gating in the store.
TaskSequencer's serialize-all-mutations concept (needs cancellation, not removal).
TTSFlightRecorder ring buffer + anomaly auto-snapshot with pre-freeze diagnostics callback (fix its thread topology, keep the tool).
PlatformIntegration's metadata deadband (Bluetooth flicker) and MediaSessionManager's perceptual-palette conic progress artwork overlay.
The detached-persistence WebKit IDB-hang workaround behavior (preserve it when persistence moves behind a port).
targetDesign: Single production topology: worker-only engine behind a standalone TtsEngine interface (commands are acks; all state flows through one monotonic, immutable PlaybackSnapshot stream with error/download fields). Decompose AudioPlayerService into a PlaybackController (FSM + TaskSequencer upgraded with epoch/AbortSignal cancellation and watchdogs — the only component allowed to change status), an immutable QueueModel (current PlaybackStateManager minus persistence), a SessionStore port (dbService TTS state + Yjs position, owning the WebKit-detach policy), AnalysisApplier (masks/adaptations as sequenced commands), MediaMetadataPublisher (single metadata/position builder with deadbands), and DragnetGesture (gesture detection internal to the engine, eliminating the UI's clearPauseGesture coupling). Split useTTSStore into useTTSSettingsStore (persisted, replicated as an explicit data-only TTSSettingsData payload) and useTTSPlaybackStore (engine mirror, never replicated), killing the echo loop while keeping the replicationSpec machinery intact. TTSQueueItem/TTSStatus move to src/types/tts.ts. TTSProviderManager keeps the PlaybackBackend role with one fallback path emitting a providerChanged event, capability interfaces for voice download, and listener disposal. Flight recorder gains a worker-side export API consumed by DiagnosticsTab via the engine handle. The expanded parity scenario suite (restore, masks, adaptations, navigation, dragnet, fallback) becomes the contract spine, absorbing and deleting the 12 per-bug regression files; vi.mock is banned inside the engine directory in favor of the fakes. SyncEngine is deleted or finally wired to a real highlight feature.

overview: The path from EPUB XHTML to synthesized speech, in two halves. Ingestion (src/lib/tts.ts + src/lib/offscreen-renderer.ts): renders spine items in a hidden epubjs rendition, walks the DOM, suppresses/captures citation markers, segments via Intl.Segmenter (TextSegmenter), sanitizes (processors/Sanitizer), maps segments to CFIs, persists sentences + markers + table snapshots. Playback (AudioContentPipeline, 891 lines): loads persisted sentences, re-refines against user settings (abbreviation/length merging via TextScanningTrie caches), builds the TTS queue, and fires background GenAI analyses — reference-section skip masks (deterministic detector + Gemini with telemetry) and table-image narrative adaptations (TableAdaptationProcessor). At speak time, LexiconService assembles user + 403 built-in Bible rules (src/data/bible-lexicon.ts, 2899 lines) which worker-safe LexiconApplier applies as sequential regexes; cloud providers cache audio via TTSCache keyed on processed text. All host access goes through the well-designed EngineContext port.

debts.title: NFKD normalization after offset bookkeeping corrupts CFIs for non-ASCII books
debts.fix: Segment raw text and normalize only output segment text (or keep a per-node raw↔normalized offset map). Add composed-accent/ligature offset regression test. Ship with extractionVersion stamp + background re-ingestion for affected books.

debts.title: Escaped template literal in preprocessTableRoots emits literal 'epubcfi(${range.parent})'
debts.fix: Delete TableAdaptationProcessor.preprocessTableRoots; use cfi-utils preprocessBlockRoots. Add a range-CFI round-trip test.

debts.title: AudioContentPipeline god class (6 concerns incl. UI-store write and 125 lines of telemetry)
debts.fix: Split into SectionQueueBuilder (pure), ReferenceSectionDetector (strategy: deterministic | Gemini, owns retry), CfiGrouper (on cfi-utils), injected DetectionTelemetry observer; move title resolution into lib/reader/titleResolver and the readerUI write into the host.

debts.title: Citation markers dropped on the primary analysis path — persisted results are path-dependent
debts.fix: Pass {sentences, citationMarkers} together or always fetch markers when absent; test asserting marker hints present when invoked from loadSection.

debts.title: Lexicon assembly cache never populated for books; 403 Bible rules rebuilt per call; stale rules mid-playback
debts.severity: high (overstated: Cited code is real: getRules' bookId branch returns at LexiconService.ts:114 before the memo write (:141-146), 403 bible rules are rebuilt per miss, and rule edits don't invalidate activeLexiconRules.)
debts.fix: Write cache before the book-path return; compile Bible ruleset once into a frozen module-level array; CompiledLexicon value object keyed by (bookId, language, store version) invalidated via useLexiconStore.subscribe.

debts.title: Bible lexicon hardwired across layers with three sources of truth for one flag
debts.fix: Single resolveBiblePreference() used by both sites; delete the pushed singleton boolean (read via port); extract data to lazy-loaded JSON behind a SystemLexiconProvider interface (makes domain lexicons pluggable).

debts.title: src/lib/tts.ts: live ingestion extractor mislocated as a grab-bag the engine reverse-imports; double refinement bakes merges into persisted data
debts.fix: Move shared types to tts/types.ts; relocate extractor to lib/ingestion/sentenceExtractor.ts; persist RAW sentences and refine exclusively at playback (behind extractionVersion).

debts.fix: One ensureGenAIReady(ctx) + resolveSectionTitle(structure, sectionId) helper; gate mock seam behind import.meta.env.DEV; single model constant or drop the fallback (configure is the host's job per the port contract).

debts.title: Test sprawl: 61 files, one-bug-one-file pattern, embedded AI deliberation comments

debts.fix: Drop Sanitizer's citation regexes (DOM suppression is better-informed; verify with the citation-skipping integration test); surface processInitialisms as a toggleable system rule visible in applyLexiconWithTrace; apply destructive sanitization at playback.

debts.fix: Logger sweep, re-indent, named ReferenceDetectionOutcome type, deterministic/localized messages, provider-registry-derived cost table, honest CSV header (parse accepts both), normalizeLanguageCode before profile lookup.

Direct dependency on epubjs internals via 'epubjs/src/epubcfi' with @ts-expect-error on compare() (AudioContentPipeline.ts:5,670; TableAdaptationProcessor.ts:2,235,237; cfi-utils.ts:4).
localStorage.getItem('mockGenAIResponse') test seam compiled into production GenAI gating (AudioContentPipeline.ts:473, TableAdaptationProcessor.ts:77).
keepers: EngineContext port architecture (src/lib/tts/engine/EngineContext.ts): one explicit boundary, type-derived store snapshots with zero runtime imports, Zustand/worker/fake implementations; AudioContentPipeline and TableAdaptationProcessor honor it via required constructor injection — preserve and extend.
LexiconApplier/LexiconService split: pure worker-safe rule application vs yjs-backed CRUD, with documented rationale (LexiconApplier.ts:1-12).
TextScanningTrie: allocation-free, boundary-verified forward/reverse matching with lookup tables and unit + fuzz coverage.
Dynamic refinement concept — persist sentences once, re-merge against current settings at playback with reference-equality trie caches (keep the concept; fix the ingest-time pre-merge).
Citation-marker capture (tts.ts:73-156): rich metadata (super/numeric/glued/leading/targetHref, MathML guard) validated by a real-EPUB integration test covering three publisher markup styles.
Promise-dedup + persisted retry/timeout state for GenAI analyses (AudioContentPipeline.ts:421-459) — sound pattern, just needs extraction.
Deterministic shadow detector + telemetry loop for offline threshold tuning — keep the capability, relocate it out of the god class.
Content-addressed audio caching on processed text (BaseCloudProvider.getOrFetch + TTSCache) — invalidation-correct by construction with in-flight dedup.
Offscreen extraction through the same epubjs pipeline the reader uses (CFI parity), with XSS serialize hook and sandbox patching.
CFI merge fast/slow paths (tryFastMergeCfi → mergeCfiSlow) and per-locale Intl.Segmenter cache.
targetDesign: Two cleanly versioned stages. Ingestion: EpubRenderer → SentenceExtractor (relocated from src/lib/tts.ts, with CitationMarkerDetector as its own module) persists RAW, offset-correct sentences + markers + table snapshots stamped with extractionVersion; normalization happens per text node with an explicit raw↔normalized offset map so CFIs are exact. Playback (worker-capable, all host access via EngineContext): SectionQueueBuilder (pure sentences+settings→queue, no UI writes — returns {queue,title} and the host updates UI), SegmentRefiner (existing TextSegmenter, now the ONLY refinement pass so every setting is retroactive), ReferenceSectionDetector as a strategy interface (deterministic always-available fallback, Gemini enhancer, telemetry as injected observer, retry/dedup in a small AnalysisCache), TableAdapter on shared CFI utilities, and a LexiconEngine: LexiconAssembler builds a CompiledLexicon value object (rules+order+language+version) cached by store version and invalidated by subscription; Bible data becomes a lazy-loaded JSON behind a SystemLexiconProvider interface (pluggable domain lexicons); one resolveBiblePreference function replaces the store→singleton push; processInitialisms becomes a visible, toggleable system rule appearing in the trace UI. All CFI string mechanics (cfiContains, stripCfiWrapper, preprocessBlockRoots) live solely in cfi-utils with one canonical separator set. Dead lexiconHash machinery removed; audio cache stays keyed on processed text. Tests consolidate to one behavioral spec per module plus fuzz/perf suffix files, standardized on FakeEngineContext.

overview: The synthesis layer of the audiobook feature: five providers behind one ITTSProvider interface — WebSpeechProvider and CapacitorTTSProvider (device engines, both id 'local'), PiperProvider (local WASM neural TTS with HuggingFace model downloads via piper-utils and a postinstall-patched worker), and GoogleTTSProvider/OpenAIProvider/LemonFoxProvider (cloud REST, extending BaseCloudProvider which adds IndexedDB caching + in-flight request dedup). TTSProviderManager (src/lib/tts/TTSProviderManager.ts) holds the active provider, normalizes events, and implements cloud→local fallback; it is the production PlaybackBackend for the engine. providerFactory.ts is the single construction point (provider ids cross the worker boundary as strings). useTTSStore (528 lines) persists provider id, API keys, and per-language voice profiles while also mirroring live playback state; useTTS is a small mount hook. Key files: src/lib/tts/providers/*, TTSProviderManager.ts, providerFactory.ts, src/store/useTTSStore.ts, scripts/patch_piper_worker.js.

debts.title: Speed applied at both synthesis and playback; semantics undefined
debts.fix: Synthesize at 1.0 always; apply rate at the sink after src load (set defaultPlaybackRate+playbackRate); remove speed from cloud request bodies and from the cache key; add non-1.0-speed tests per provider.

debts.title: Cloud-failure fallback double-fires and races the task sequencer
debts.fix: Providers signal failure exactly once (reject only); collapse manager handlers into one; fallback becomes a single sequenced engine task: stop → awaited swap → replay current item, with retry cap.

debts.title: No provider registry — adding a provider edits ~6 hand-maintained sites
debts.fix: ProviderDescriptor registry ({id, displayName, kind, requiresApiKey, capabilities, build(ctx)}) as single source of truth; derive unions via 'as const'; render settings UI from the registry.

debts.title: useTTSStore god store with dual voice-settings representation
debts.fix: Split into persisted useTTSSettingsStore (profiles as the only representation, selectors for flat values, one selectVoiceForLanguage()) and ephemeral useTTSPlaybackStore; move engine sync into a controller.

debts.title: API key keystroke rebuilds provider, stops playback, hits network
debts.fix: Buffer keystrokes; re-init on blur/explicit save with debounce; add a deliberate 'test key' action.

debts.title: Piper not offline-capable: HF catalog fetch every session + ONNX runtime from CDN
debts.fix: Cache voices.json (stale-while-revalidate) and enumerate downloaded voices offline; vendor onnxruntime-web into public/piper and precache via SW; remove the CDN default.

debts.title: piper-utils module-global singleton: pinned model blobs, eternal promise chain, per-call onmessage swaps
debts.fix: Encapsulate in a PiperRuntime class with request-id message protocol, LRU blob eviction, error-resetting queue, and dispose().

debts.title: TTSProviderManager: stale-provider event leaks, no dispose, 'as any' piper API, throwaway construction
debts.fix: Add dispose() + unsubscribe to the provider contract; manager detaches/disposes outgoing providers and injects one shared AudioSink; replace duck typing with a VoiceDownloadable capability interface from the registry; distinct ids for webspeech/capacitor.

debts.title: ITTSProvider contract inconsistent and partly dead
debts.fix: Shrink interface to what is called; specify play() semantics; add a shared cross-provider contract test suite; type the error event.

debts.title: Dead settings and dead cost machinery
debts.fix: Delete or implement each: wire cost warnings end-to-end or remove the toggle+estimator; implement pitch or drop it from profiles; remove the mark-less timepoint request.

debts.title: No cancellation or timeout on cloud fetches; stop() can't abort
debts.fix: Per-request AbortController in the registry; stop() aborts non-preload requests; default timeout in fetchAudio used by all subclasses.

debts.title: TTS audio cache (cache_audio_blobs) never evicted
debts.fix: LRU eviction by lastAccessed with a byte budget in MaintenanceService (timestamps already exist).

debts.title: Piper worker patching: string surgery on a gitignored artifact with warn-and-continue failures
debts.fix: Vendor the patched worker + artifacts in-repo (recorded upstream version), delete the postinstall patcher or fail install on anchor mismatch, add a smoke test that the served worker contains the clamp patch.

debts.title: Import cycle store→engine→backend→factory→store with init-time side effects
debts.fix: Pass {apiKey, activeLanguage} into buildProviderById as parameters (call sites already have store access), move rehydrate side effects to an explicit initialize().

debts.title: Hardcoded voice catalogs and language policy buried in providers
debts.fix: Voice catalogs as data (static JSON or remote-with-cache); language list derived from available voices + library languages; model selection in registry descriptor.

debts.title: API keys plaintext in localStorage
debts.fix: Document the tradeoff; exclude keys from export/sync surfaces; consider IndexedDB with non-extractable wrapping.

couplings: providerFactory.ts:12,22 reaches into src/store/useTTSStore for API keys + language, creating the useTTSStore→mainThreadAudioPlayer→TTSProviderManager→providerFactory→useTTSStore import cycle
BaseCloudProvider → TTSCache → dbService (db subsystem): providers write IndexedDB directly via the global singleton; nobody owns cache policy (no eviction, key shape split across layers)
BaseCloudProvider.ts:95 → CostEstimator → useCostStore: synthesis layer writes a Zustand store with zero readers, and counts free Piper synthesis as cloud cost

BaseCloudProvider.getOrFetch pipeline: permanent cache → in-flight request registry → owner-only tracking with finally-cleanup (BaseCloudProvider.ts:74-116), well covered by BaseCloudProvider.registry.test.ts
AudioSink abstraction with injectable sink in every provider constructor — providers testable without jsdom media shims (FakeAudioSink)
Capacitor 'Smart Handoff' gapless playback (preload via native queue + promise adoption with utterance-id guards, CapacitorTTSProvider.ts:65-153) and its thorough behavioral test suite
Transactional Piper voice download: stage → commit → verification load → rollback (PiperProvider.ts:158-197)
CJK-aware text chunking with clause/lexical/hard-split fallback (PiperProvider.ts:26-67) and chunk-offset-aware WAV stitching (piper-utils.ts:199-268)
TTSCache SHA-256 content-keyed IndexedDB caching (modulo removing speed from the key)
WebSpeechProvider voiceschanged race handling with timeout + dual listener fallback (WebSpeechProvider.ts:24-68)
Provider unit tests are behavioral contracts, not single-bug regressions — unusually healthy for this codebase
flightRecorder instrumentation in the Capacitor provider
targetDesign: 1) Provider registry as single source of truth: PROVIDERS: ProviderDescriptor[] with {id, displayName, kind: device|wasm|cloud, requiresApiKey, capabilities: {downloadableVoices, alignment, synthesisSpeed, pitch}, build(ctx)}; ctx = {apiKey?, language, sink} passed in (kills the store import cycle); all TTSProviderId unions derived 'as const'; settings UI rendered from the registry. 2) Narrow, specified ITTSProvider: {id, init, getVoices, play, preload, pause, stop, dispose, typed events with unsubscribe}; optional VoiceDownloadable/LocaleAware capability interfaces reached via descriptor-driven type guards — no 'as any', no dead members; one shared cross-provider contract test suite pinning play() semantics and single-shot error signaling. 3) One failure path: providers reject play() only; TTSProviderManager becomes a dumb holder (registry construction, detach+dispose outgoing provider, one shared AudioSink); fallback is a sequenced engine task (stop → awaited swap → replay) with retry cap. 4) Defined speed policy: synthesize at 1.0, rate applied by the sink after source load, cache key = hash(text|voiceId|providerId); pitch/volume implemented end-to-end or deleted. 5) Store split: persisted useTTSSettingsStore (profiles map only, selectors derive flat values; debounced key edits) + ephemeral useTTSPlaybackStore; engine sync in one controller. 6) Piper self-containment: PiperRuntime class (request-id protocol, model-blob LRU, dispose), offline voice enumeration from cached models, vendored onnxruntime + patched worker in-repo, postinstall patching deleted. 7) Audio-cache LRU eviction in MaintenanceService.

overview: The presentation layer for TTS playback: UnifiedAudioPanel (in-reader "Audio Deck" sheet with transport, queue, voice settings), ReaderTTSController (render-null controller painting the spoken-sentence highlight into epub.js), TTSQueue/TTSQueueItem, the floating bottom pill (CompassPill, 828 lines in components/ui, rendered by ReaderControlBar from RootLayout), TTSSettingsTab (props-driven global settings tab), TTSAbbreviationSettings (segmentation rules), LexiconManager (681-line pronunciation-rule dialog, mounted in 3 places), and a fully dead src/components/audio/ directory (AudioReaderHUD + SatelliteFAB). State flows engine→useTTSStore→components via one subscription set up in useTTSStore.initialize(); commands flow through store actions, except 7 call sites that bypass the store and hit the getAudioPlayer() singleton directly. Hooks useTTS and useSectionDuration provide queue-sync and time-remaining estimation.

debts.fix: Single instance driven by store state {open, initialTerm} with an openLexicon(term?) action; remove the other mounts

(1) One command facade: useTTSStore (or a useAudioCommands hook over it) exposes the complete command surface — play/pause/stop/seekSentence/skipSection/jumpTo/preview/loadSection/setBookId/clearPauseGesture — and an ESLint no-restricted-imports rule bans mainThreadAudioPlayer from components/hooks; engine-side settings reads move to a narrow TtsSettingsSource interface so the store stops being a two-way coupling point. overview: ~22 Zustand stores form Versicle's source of truth. Nine are bridged to a singleton Y.Doc (src/store/yjs-provider.ts) via a forked zustand-middleware-yjs: useBookStore ('library'), useReadingStateStore ('progress'), useAnnotationStore, usePreferencesStore (one map per device), useReadingListStore, useVocabularyStore, useLexiconStore, useContentAnalysisStore, useDeviceStore. Five persist to localStorage (useTTSStore, useGenAIStore, useDriveStore, useLocalHistoryStore, useGoogleServicesStore; plus useSyncStore in lib/sync). The rest are ephemeral UI stores (useUIStore, useReaderUIStore, useToastStore, useBackNavigationStore, plus useSidebarStore and useCostStore hidden outside src/store). yjs-provider owns schema version 5, migrations, and obsolete-client quarantine. selectors.ts (416 ln) builds the library view model with hand-rolled render-time caching. useLibraryStore (798 ln) is really an import/restore/offload workflow engine, not a store.

targetDesign: Three explicit store tiers (synced / local-persisted / ephemeral) declared in a src/store/registry.ts, with all stores (incl. useSyncStore, useSidebarStore, useCostStore) moved under src/store. Fork surgery on zustand-middleware-yjs: syncedKeys whitelist (kills the popover leak), default-merge hydration instead of replace-with-delete (kills the undefined-state class and the migration-as-backfill hack), per-key scoped diffing (kills page-turn write amplification), and a whenHydrated() promise. Schema version moves to a dedicated 'meta' Y.Map checked synchronously by FirestoreSyncManager before applying remote updates; migrations become pure transforms run exactly once by a coordinator after hydration, atomic with the version bump, failing loudly into safe mode. Stores become dumb caches: a LibraryService owns import/restore/offload/remove serialized by a per-book keyed mutex; a ProgressService owns the single write path with the reading-list projection as one subscription; useTTSStore splits into persisted settings vs ephemeral playback mirror with commands on the engine handle. One pure progress-resolution module replaces the four copies. The library view model is derived outside render via store subscriptions, replacing the 220-line module cache. Hygiene: delete use-local-storage + 7 tests, syncState, index.ts; consolidate race tests into service-level invariant suites; secrets in one credentials store; toast queue; debug globals DEV-gated. Migration path (v6): delete annotations.popover key, create meta map (dual-write library.__schemaVersion for one release so v5 clients quarantine), fold preferences/<deviceId> maps into a single keyed map, pre-migration checkpoint via existing CheckpointService flow.

overview: Cross-device sync layer for a local-first app. The Yjs doc (yjs-provider.ts) is source of truth; FirestoreSyncManager.ts (993 lines, singleton) attaches a forked y-cinder FireProvider to replicate it to users/{uid}/versicle/{workspaceId} in the user's own Firebase project (BYO config pasted in SyncSettingsTab). Supporting pieces: CheckpointService (rolling-10 local IDB snapshots, wipe-and-reload restore), MigrationStateService (localStorage state machine surviving the hard reload mid workspace-switch, with confirm/rollback modals and ErrorBoundary fallback), schema-version quarantine (ObsoleteLockView via middleware onObsolete), workspace CRUD with tombstone deletion, firestore.rules, device mesh (device-id.ts + useDeviceStore with 5-min heartbeat), and recovery UI (RecoverySettingsTab, CheckpointDiffView/JsonDiffViewer, DataRecoveryView). Mock transport (MockFireProvider + 8 inline isMock branches) powers all Playwright sync journeys.

targetDesign: Restructure src/lib/sync into: core/SyncOrchestrator (single lifecycle owner — boot interceptor, one syncEnabled gate, heartbeat); core/AuthSession (SocialLogin→Firebase auth); core/ProviderConnection (FireProvider attach/detach, event normalization); core/backend/SyncBackend interface with FirestoreBackend and MockBackend implementations (kills all inline __VERSICLE_MOCK_FIRESTORE__ branches — Playwright selects MockBackend at the composition root); one shared downloadWorkspaceState(path) temp-doc utility; workspaces/WorkspaceService (create/switch/delete over SyncBackend) and MigrationStateMachine with a staged-swap protocol (download → staging IDB → verify → atomic swap → reload) and pinned migration checkpoints; checkpoints/ with protected-flag pruning and navigator.locks around restore; a typed SyncEvent bus so UI (toasts, pulse indicator, lastSyncTime from flush events) subscribes instead of the manager importing useToastStore. overview: IndexedDB storage layer for Versicle (EpubLibraryDB v24 via 'idb'): STATIC domain (book manifests/EPUB blobs/structure), CACHE domain (TTS audio, TTS prep, session state, render metrics, table images), APP domain (sync checkpoints, sync log, flight snapshots). User data lives separately in Yjs (versicle-yjs DB). Key files: src/db/db.ts (connection + idempotent v24 upgrade), src/db/DBService.ts (670-line worker-safe data-access singleton with WebKit-deadlock-safe session-state mirroring), src/db/BookRepository.ts (merges IDB rows with Yjs inventory into BookMetadata), src/types/db.ts (934-line type hub, 59 importers), src/lib/idb-write-lock.ts (process-wide readwrite serializer shared with forked y-idb), src/lib/BackupService.ts (Yjs-snapshot backup/restore), src/lib/MaintenanceService.ts (orphan pruning, metadata regeneration), plus export utilities and validators.

debts.title: 'Clear All Data' leaves all user data behind (Yjs IDB never cleared)
debts.fix: Single data-layer wipeAllData(): closeDB + deleteDatabase both DBs + yjsPersistence.clearData() + localStorage/caches; UI never enumerates stores

debts.title: Cached cloud-TTS alignment silently lost (alignmentData vs alignment drift)
debts.severity: high (overstated: The field drift is real and every cited line checks out: cacheSegment writes 'alignmentData' (DBService.ts:574, CacheAudioBlob types/db.ts:296), getCachedSegment returns the raw row typed as CachedSeg)
debts.fix: Delete CachedSegment, return CacheAudioBlob with one canonical field; add cache write-read round-trip test asserting alignment survives

debts.title: Backup restore corrupts cover images (ArrayBuffer JSON-stringified to {})
debts.fix: Strip or base64 binary fields in generateManifest; merge-not-overwrite on restore; bump manifest version with sanitizing v2 reader; one-time boot repair nulling {} coverBlobs

debts.title: WebKit-deadlock write lock is per-context and per-callsite, not an invariant
debts.fix: Replace promise chain with navigator.locks (spans workers/tabs); route ALL readwrite txns through one gateway whose API takes a synchronous callback (structurally bans intra-txn awaits); lint-ban 'readwrite' transactions elsewhere

debts.title: types/db.ts is a god module with dead types and inverted dependencies
debts.fix: Split into types/storage/{static,cache,app}.ts, types/user.ts, types/sync.ts; delete dead exports; collapse duplicates; define persisted queue-item shape in cache types and invert the AudioPlayerService dependency

debts.title: BookMetadata is a legacy intersection type with everything optional
debts.fix: Explicit LibraryBook view model owned by BookRepository with availability: 'local'|'offloaded'|'ghost' discriminant and required display fields; delete Book/BookSource/BookState

debts.title: No data-access boundary: three competing tiers, raw getDB() in 7 modules
debts.fix: Single src/data/ gateway with domain repositories; getDB private to the package; ESLint no-restricted-imports bans db/db and db/DBService elsewhere

debts.title: Backup restore: unvalidated input, raw y-idb internals, magic sleeps, destructive-before-validated
debts.fix: zod-validate manifest; dry-run Y.applyUpdate on a scratch doc before any destructive step; add explicit flush()/whenSynced to the y-idb fork; remove sleeps

debts.title: Schema strategy: idempotent-only upgrade destroys straggler user data, no multi-tab/failure handling
debts.fix: Versioned migration registry; snapshot legacy user stores before deletion; blocked/blocking handlers with reload prompt; reset dbPromise on failure with bounded retry

debts.title: Unbounded caches: LRU fields exist but eviction never implemented; storage never made persistent
debts.fix: Request navigator.storage.persist() at first import; size-budgeted idle-time LRU eviction; extend orphan scan to all per-book stores; surface storage.estimate() in settings

debts.title: Three validation layers: one hand-rolled, one dead-and-drifted zod, none at trust boundaries
debts.fix: Single src/data/schemas.ts zod source of truth (z.infer row types); apply at backup restore, Android payload, Firestore inbound; delete sync/validators.ts and its fuzz tests

debts.title: Export utilities triplicated; two broken on native Android
debts.fix: Both call exportFile(); export-notes.ts becomes pure markdown formatting

debts.title: Session-state mirror correctness gaps and dead code in DBService
debts.fix: Seed-before-write always; flush-on-teardown via visibilitychange/web locks instead of dropping; single session-state owner per app instance (worker proxies via EngineContext port); delete dead branches/params

debts.title: Three parallel Yjs-snapshot capture/restore mechanisms
debts.fix: One YjsSnapshotService (capture/validate/apply with documented reload contract); Backup, Checkpoint, Android backup become thin format adapters

debts.title: Hygiene: stale README, dead app_metadata store, vacuous/slow tests
debts.fix: Rewrite README from target design; drop or repurpose app_metadata; fake timers; meaningful assertions

couplings: UI owns storage logic: GlobalSettingsDialog.tsx:246-254 hand-clears IDB stores by name (incorrectly); ContentAnalysisLegend.tsx:94, useEpubReader.ts:293/459, useSmartTOC.ts:69 call dbService directly from components/hooks

TTS engine <-> persistence bidirectional tangle: 5 TTS modules import dbService (AudioPlayerService.ts:4, PlaybackStateManager.ts:2, AudioContentPipeline.ts:1, TTSCache.ts:1, TableAdaptationProcessor.ts:1) while types/db.ts:11 imports TTSQueueItem FROM AudioPlayerService — the persisted row shape is defined by the TTS engine's biggest file; EngineContext deliberately exempts dbService from its ports, which is what breaks the cross-context write lock

Diagnostics: TTSFlightRecorder.ts:165-227 does raw getDB() CRUD on flight_snapshots outside the data layer

keepers: Three-domain IDB taxonomy (STATIC/CACHE/APP, user data exclusively in Yjs) — clean local-first shape, well documented at db.ts:22-30
Worker-safety discipline: DBService's documented yjs-free contract (DBService.ts:58-63) with main-thread merge repositories (BookRepository.ts:1-9) — keep the idea, harden enforcement

WebKit-hang engineering: in-memory mirror + debounce + single-synchronous-put + transactionRunner injection into y-idb (yjs-provider.ts:32), evidence-driven and meticulously documented — generalize, don't discard
Typed idb DBSchema (EpubLibraryDB) for compile-time store/key checking
handleDbError -> typed DatabaseError/StorageFullError surfaced at import sites and global rejection handler (App.tsx:125)
V2 backup concept using Y.encodeStateAsUpdate (CRDT-correct, mergeable) — flaws are in validation/sequencing, not the concept
Blob->ArrayBuffer write-time normalization for WebKit in ingestBook; ingestBook's single-transaction atomicity across 5 stores
Small sharp utilities: crypto.ts, json-diff.ts, platform-aware export.ts
targetDesign: src/data/ becomes the only storage subsystem. connection.ts: openDB with blocked/blocking/terminated handlers, dbPromise reset-on-failure, navigator.storage.persist(). schema.ts: typed DBSchema plus a versioned migration registry (snapshot legacy user stores before deletion). write-gate.ts: navigator.locks-based exclusive writer spanning workers and tabs, API write(stores, syncCallback) that structurally bans intra-transaction awaits; ESLint bans 'readwrite' transactions and idb imports outside src/data. rows/: zod schemas per store with z.infer row types — same schemas validate backup restore, Android payloads, and Firestore inbound. repos/: bookContent (manifests/resources/structure/ingest/offload), playbackCache (session mirror, single owner; worker engine reaches it via an EngineContext persistence port), audioCache (get/put plus size-budgeted LRU eviction job), diagnostics (flight snapshots), checkpoints (absorbs CheckpointService raw IDB). snapshot/YjsSnapshotService: one capture/validate(dry-run applyUpdate)/apply+flush implementation; BackupService, CheckpointService, android-backup become thin format adapters; y-idb fork gains explicit flush()/whenSynced. wipe.ts: wipeAllData() deleting both databases plus localStorage/caches. types/db.ts dissolves into data/rows/* and types/user.ts; persisted TTS queue-item shape lives in cache row types with AudioPlayerService importing it (dependency inverted); BookMetadata replaced by a LibraryBook view model with an availability discriminant ('local'|'offloaded'|'ghost') produced solely by BookRepository. UI/hooks import repositories only — never dbService or getDB.

overview: The pipeline turning an EPUB (file picker, drag-drop, ZIP, Google Drive) into a library entry: src/lib/ingestion.ts (extractBookData/extractBookMetadata/reprocessBook), offscreen-renderer.ts (hidden-iframe epubjs rendering for sentence/CFI extraction, table screenshots, font metrics), cover-palette.ts (two K-means palette extractors), BookImportService.ts (facade over DBService.ingestBook), batch-ingestion.ts (ZIP expansion + loop). Orchestration actually lives in src/store/useLibraryStore.ts (798 lines): duplicate detection, ghost-book matching, overwrite merge, hydration. UI: src/components/library/ (LibraryView 679 lines plus FileUploader, BookCard/Cover/ListItem, six dialogs) and the reading list (filename-keyed Yjs store, CSV import/export, fuzzy entity-resolution joins in selectors.ts). Static data goes to IndexedDB static_*/cache_* stores; user data to Yjs.

debts.title: Batch import bypasses duplicate/ghost detection and silently drops failures
debts.fix: Single import pipeline with an identify+policy stage; batch = N jobs through it; surface per-file success/duplicate/failure in ImportProgressUI.

debts.title: Extraction pipeline triplicated; every import parses the EPUB twice and runs the cover pipeline twice
debts.fix: One extractBook(file, depth: 'metadata'|'full', signal) function; reprocessBook = extract(existing blob)+persist; chapter mapping as a shared pure function.

debts.title: useLibraryStore.addBook is a ~300-line god action with copy-pasted race guards
debts.fix: Extract an ImportOrchestrator service with an explicit job queue and per-job progress; reduce the store to UI projection; CAS-style guards in one owner.

debts.title: BookExtractionData user-domain outputs are dead code, and the live replacements drop perceptualPalette and language from the synced inventory
debts.fix: Registration step consumes data.inventory from the extractor (one producer); delete unused outputs; one-time backfill of perceptualPalette/language into Yjs from local manifests.

debts.title: Two competing book identities (filename vs UUID) reconciled by render-time fuzzy matching
debts.fix: Add optional bookId FK to ReadingListEntry, resolved once at import/CSV-link time (fuzzy matcher becomes the one-time resolver); one-time migration pass linking existing entries.

debts.title: ReprocessingInterstitial can start overlapping reprocess runs of the same book
debts.fix: In-flight guard keyed by bookId (or route reprocess through the import queue); drop onComplete from effect deps; memoize the callback in LibraryView.

debts.title: fileHash is a filename-embedding djb2 fingerprint, not the documented SHA-256; renamed-file restores fail
debts.fix: Store a real SHA-256 contentHash excluding filename; restore accepts contentHash OR legacy fingerprint minus filename; lazy manifest upgrade.

debts.title: Import UX implemented twice with divergent capabilities per entry point
debts.fix: One useImportController hook (selection, validation, dup dialog queue) shared by all surfaces; every entry point accepts multi-file + ZIP.

debts.title: Reading-list CSV export duplicated with incompatible schema; plus cover-gradient/duration helpers duplicated across components
debts.fix: Delete the bespoke exporter, call exportReadingListToCSV; extract shared book-display helpers module.

debts.title: Circular lib/store layering; worker-bundle safety enforced only by docstring
debts.fix: Enforce components→stores→services→db with lint rules (no-restricted-imports/dependency-cruiser); services return results instead of mutating stores.

debts.title: Extraction is main-thread, uncancellable, with global single-flight progress
debts.fix: Import job queue with AbortSignal threaded through extract/persist; stream ZIP entries one EPUB at a time; per-job progress.

debts.title: Type-safety erosion: useAllBooks returns any[], allProgress smuggled via casts
debts.fix: Declare a LibraryBook return type; move the 5-store merge into a derived store to delete hand-rolled memoization; one typed epubjs adapter.

debts.title: Dead code and dead parameters in library UI
debts.fix: Delete dead exports/params; either implement CFI-targeted resume or simplify onResume to (book)=>void.

debts.title: Docs drifted from reality
debts.fix: Regenerate docs post-refactor; delete per-directory READMEs that restate filenames.

debts.title: Static-metadata hydration lifecycle lives inside LibraryView
debts.fix: Hydrate at app bootstrap via a subscription on Yjs inventory key deltas, independent of views.

TTS settings leak into the import path: useTTSStore.getState() pulled at 5 call sites (useLibraryStore.ts:304,426,473,581,734) to build ExtractionOptions; persisted cache_tts_preparation depends on TTS settings with no invalidation when they change
Reader subsystem calls reprocessBook directly (src/components/reader/ContentAnalysisLegend.tsx:272), parallel to the library's ReprocessingInterstitial path

keepers: v18 static/user/cache domain split: ingestBook writes only static_*+cache_*, deleteBook leaves Yjs user data for soft-delete ghost books (DBService.ts:180-329) — sound model, preserve
Ghost-book design: Yjs inventory snapshots (title/author/packed 5x16-bit palette) render full libraries on devices without binaries

Offscreen-renderer fidelity strategy: extracting sentences/CFIs from a real rendered epubjs iframe so TTS CFIs match playback, with sanitizer hook on spine serialize (offscreen-renderer.ts:192-200) and iframe sandbox patching
WebKit IndexedDB discipline: Blob→ArrayBuffer before transactions, reads hoisted out of readwrite txs, documented with bug history (ingestion.ts:155-168, DBService.ts:186-210) — preserve verbatim
cancellable-task-runner: well-documented, well-tested generator runner — keep and wire into the import pipeline
cover-palette.ts math (deterministic K-means, CIELAB, salience scoring): pure, heavily tested; only its plumbing is broken
entity-resolution normalization functions (as a one-time linker, not a render-time join)
BookImportService as a worker-bundle firewall concept — keep, add lint enforcement
Delete/Offload/Replace dialogs: clean, focused, accessible components
targetDesign: A features/library module with strict layering (components → stores → services → db, lint-enforced). Core: an ImportOrchestrator service owning a queue of ImportJobs — the only entry point for single, batch, ZIP, Drive, restore, and reprocess flows. Pipeline stages per job: validate → identify (real SHA-256 content hash + filename) → resolve policy (new | duplicate→ask/replace | ghost→adopt existing id) → extract (ONE extractBook(file, depth, AbortSignal) function with a metadata-only short-circuit; reprocess reuses it) → persist (single transaction via DBService) → register (inventory + reading-list entry consumed from the extractor's output, including perceptualPalette/language, applied atomically per job). useLibraryStore shrinks to UI projection: per-job progress, staticMetadata cache, offloaded id-set; hydration moves to app bootstrap driven by Yjs inventory key deltas. ReadingListEntry gains an optional bookId FK resolved once at import/CSV-link time, demoting generateMatchKey from render-time join to one-time resolver; selectors become typed (LibraryBook) and lose their any-typed module caches by moving the 5-store merge into a derived store. UI consolidates on a single useImportController hook (file selection, drop, validation, duplicate dialog queue) powering LibraryView, EmptyLibrary, and Drive import, with all entry points supporting multi-file + ZIP; shared book-display helpers (gradient, duration) extracted. ZIP expansion streams one EPUB at a time. SW cover route becomes a shared constant. Per-file batch results (imported/duplicate/failed) surfaced in ImportProgressUI.

overview: In-book full-text search: a 147-line SearchEngine class (in-memory Map<bookId, Map<href, text>>, case-insensitive indexOf scan, ±40-char excerpts, 50-result cap) exposed over Comlink from a 5-line worker entry (src/workers/search.worker.ts). A singleton SearchClient (src/lib/search.ts) lazily spawns the worker, extracts spine-item text from the epubjs archive (DOMParser on main thread) in batches of 5 with single-flight dedup and progress broadcast, and is terminated on ReaderView unmount. SearchPanel.tsx triggers indexing when the search sidebar opens and renders results; clicking navigates by chapter href plus a 500ms-delayed text scroll. Library search (LibrarySearchBar + LibraryView filter) and notes search are unrelated metadata filters. History: FlexSearch → escaped-RegExp → indexOf; docs and several tests still describe the dead generations.

debts.title: Worker-side XML parsing path is dead in production browsers
debts.fix: Delete supportsXmlParsing, the xml field, and the worker parse branch. Either accept main-thread parsing explicitly, or truly offload by sending raw bytes to the worker with a worker-compatible text extractor (tag-stripper suffices for search text).

debts.title: Result navigation cannot target a specific match; cfi field dead; 500ms magic timeout
debts.fix: Record per-occurrence char offsets at index time and resolve to CFIs (epub.js section.cfiFromRange) eagerly or on click; navigate by CFI with a temporary highlight annotation; delete scrollToText and the timer.

debts.title: Excerpt index misalignment when lowercasing changes string length
debts.fix: Match against the original string using an escaped-literal regex with 'giu' flags (no backtracking risk for escaped literals), or keep an offset map; strengthen the existing test to assert exact excerpt boundaries.

debts.title: Stale identity: docs and tests describe two previous engine generations
debts.fix: Rewrite the three doc locations to describe the lowercase-indexOf scan; delete or rewrite the zero-width RegExp test.

debts.title: Index rebuilt from the zip every reader session; no persistence
debts.fix: Persist extracted plain text per book in IndexedDB via DBService (new store, deleted with the book); index load becomes read-rows-and-post; invalidate on delete/re-import.

debts.title: No worker failure handling; client cache diverges; terminate() leaks in-flight work
debts.fix: Add onerror handler that resets client state and surfaces a toast; make terminate() reject/abort pending operations via a generation counter or AbortSignal checked per batch; clear caches unconditionally.

debts.title: Module-level singleton with split-brain lifecycle ownership
debts.fix: Reader-session-scoped SearchSession created/disposed by the reader container, provided via context; constructor-inject a worker factory so tests run the real engine over MessageChannel (pattern already proven in WorkerTtsEngine.test.ts:42-44).

debts.title: Test sprawl: 8 files, ~990 lines, overlapping and partially vacuous
debts.fix: Consolidate to an engine suite (behavior + seeded fuzz) and a client/session suite (lifecycle, concurrency, failure modes) on a shared real-engine-over-MessageChannel harness; fold repros in as named regression cases; delete or budget-assert the perf file.

debts.title: Untyped epubjs access inlined in SearchClient
debts.fix: One typed epubjs adapter module (spine items, archive access, section load) shared by search and TTS extraction; remove the casts.

debts.title: Three hand-rolled search inputs; library/notes/in-book search share nothing
debts.fix: Extract a shared SearchInput component; keep metadata filtering as-is; optionally build library-wide search on the persisted text cache later.

debts.title: Silent truncation and result-list paper cuts
debts.fix: Return {results, truncated}; key by href+offset; include section titles via TOC lookup; normalize excerpt whitespace; toast on indexing failure.

couplings: ReaderView and SearchPanel split lifecycle ownership of the searchClient singleton: SearchPanel creates the index (SearchPanel.tsx:36-65) while ReaderView destroys it (ReaderView.tsx:24, 531) — coordination happens through a module-level global (src/lib/search.ts:213).

Search result navigation depends on reader-internal DOM details: ReaderView.tsx:1360-1366 glues results to rendering with a 500ms setTimeout and scrollToText (ReaderView.tsx:917-974), which uses non-standard iframe window.find() plus a single-text-node TreeWalker fallback.

SearchClient reaches into untyped epubjs fork internals: (book.spine as any).items, item: any, doc as any (src/lib/search.ts:106,115,145); the same spine-poking pattern exists in TTS content extraction with no shared typed adapter (epubjs is pinned/overridden in package.json:118).

Documentation couplings are broken: architecture.md:19,406-410 describes RegExp scanning and worker XML offload (both false), and src/workers/README.md:7 claims the engine wraps FlexSearch (removed two engine generations ago in commit 008a8675).
keepers: Comlink as the uniform worker transport — search.worker.ts is 5 lines of pure wiring and tts.worker.ts:9 explicitly mirrors it; no hand-rolled postMessage protocol exists in this subsystem.
SearchEngine as a plain environment-agnostic class, directly instantiable in tests and on either thread.
Lazy deferred indexing — cost paid only when the user opens the search panel (SearchPanel.tsx:36-65, commit f875a183).
Single-flight concurrent indexing with progress broadcast to all callers (search.ts:51-87, commit b2919d1f).
Batched extraction with awaited worker acks and main-thread yields (search.ts:108-183).

The indexOf-over-lowercased-text algorithm itself: right scale for per-book search (~10M chars in tens of ms), zero ReDoS surface after the project twice fought query-as-regex bugs (commits 3d1781e8, 2e2da045); do not reintroduce FlexSearch or an inverted index.
Two-tier extraction strategy: archive direct read with rendering fallback (search.ts:119-152).
SearchPanel hygiene: stale-request counter (SearchPanel.tsx:67-97), mounted-flag pattern, and genuinely good a11y (progressbar attrs, aria-live).
Seeded deterministic fuzz infrastructure (src/test/fuzz-utils.ts) — reuse in the consolidated suite.
targetDesign: Keep the proven core (plain SearchEngine class, Comlink transport, indexOf scan, lazy indexing) and rebuild the edges. (1) BookTextExtractor: one typed-epubjs-adapter-backed service that turns a book into {href, title, text, char offsets}, persisted in a new IndexedDB store via DBService and deleted with the book — indexing becomes read-rows-and-post instead of unzip+parse every session, and it doubles as the substrate for optional library-wide search. (2) SearchEngine fixes: match against the original string with an escaped-literal Unicode regex (kills the case-fold excerpt misalignment), return {results, truncated} with section titles and per-occurrence offsets/CFIs; delete the dead worker-XML-parsing path and supportsXmlParsing negotiation entirely. (3) Replace the module singleton with a reader-session-scoped SearchSession owning worker lifecycle (create on reader mount, dispose on unmount), with worker.onerror recovery, abortable in-flight indexing, and an injected worker factory so tests run the real engine over MessageChannel (the WorkerTtsEngine.test.ts pattern). (4) Navigation by CFI with a temporary highlight annotation, deleting scrollToText and the 500ms timer. (5) Shared SearchInput component across SearchPanel/LibrarySearchBar/NotesSearchBar. (6) Tests consolidated into an engine behavioral+fuzz suite and a session lifecycle suite; delete vacuous/duplicate files. (7) Correct architecture.md and src/workers/README.md. Explicit non-goals: no inverted index, no FlexSearch revival, no query-derived regexes. No user-data migration needed; the IDB text cache is additive and lazily populated.

overview: Three stacked layers. (1) Auth: GoogleIntegrationManager (src/lib/google/GoogleIntegrationManager.ts) picks Web/Android "strategies" wrapping @capgo/capacitor-social-login to mint OAuth access tokens per service (drive/identity, src/lib/google/config.ts); connection flags persist in useGoogleServicesStore. (2) Drive: DriveService (REST client with 401-retry and pagination), DriveScannerService (scan linked folder, index into useDriveStore, diff vs library, import via useLibraryStore.addBook), plus picker/import UI under src/components/drive/. (3) GenAI: GenAIService singleton (440 lines) wrapping Gemini with model-rotation retry and JSON-schema structured output, powering smart TOC (useSmartTOC), reference-section detection and table-image narration (consumed by TTS via EngineContext ports), and reading-list mapping (SmartLinkDialog). Expensive analysis results persist in the Yjs-synced useContentAnalysisStore, adapted for the TTS worker boundary by ContentAnalysisRepository. Config/logs live in useGenAIStore (localStorage).

overview: Mandarin-learner features: per-character pinyin overlay, Simplified→Traditional conversion, offline CC-CEDICT dictionary lookup, and a Yjs-synced known-character vocabulary that suppresses pinyin ("Smart Pinyin"). Activated per book by UserInventoryItem.language === 'zh'. Dedicated files are small — src/lib/chinese/ChineseTextProcessor.ts (lazy opencc-js/pinyin-pro wrapper), src/components/reader/PinyinOverlay.tsx (geometry overlay portal), src/hooks/useChineseDictionary.ts (14MB cedict.json loader), src/store/useVocabularyStore.ts, plus prefs in usePreferencesStore and UI in VisualSettings. But the two hardest parts live inline in god files: the pinyin/traditional engine is a closure inside useEpubReader.ts (599-699), and the vocab-triage UI is embedded in the 828-line CompassPill.tsx. useLexiconStore is TTS-owned; Chinese touches it only via LexiconRule.language scoping. Plan docs (plan/zh-*.md) describe a different shipped implementation than reality.

targetDesign: A self-contained src/features/chinese/ module with one narrow, typed seam to the reader. engine/: pure PinyinGeometryEngine (code-point safe, returns PinyinPosition[]), TraditionalConverter (length-guarded mutate/restore with cancellation), and a ChineseContentProcessor facade keyed per section. dictionary/: an offline-first DictionaryService (IndexedDB import or prefix-sharded fetch, SW runtime cache, status/error surface) plus the pure compound-lookup helper. vocabulary/: the Yjs store with simplified-canonical keys plus a minimal Character Vault UI. ui/: PinyinOverlay, VocabTriageCard (extracted from CompassPill), ChineseReadingSettings (extracted from VisualSettings). The reader core exposes a generic ContentProcessor registry and an overlay slot; the Chinese module registers itself only when getBookBaseLanguage(book) === 'zh' and the reader has zero imports from the feature. Position state lives in the module as Map<sectionId, PinyinPosition[]>, merged for the overlay and invalidated on the relocated/resize/destroy events the reader already emits — eliminating the replace-not-merge and stale-geometry bugs structurally. book.language is normalized once at the useBookStore write boundary; all consumers (Chinese module, TTS profiles, lexicon, segmenter) compare plain ISO 639-1 codes. The 14MB cedict.json leaves git; the compile script becomes a fatal-on-failure CI step with provenance stamping. CompassPill shrinks to a variant router.

targetDesign: (1) Boot: src/app/boot.ts — explicit async sequencer with typed phases (migration-halt | restoring | loading | ready | failed); subsystems register boot tasks so App.tsx (≤100 lines) stops importing sync/drive/TTS internals. (2) Routing: /, /notes, /read/:id, /settings/:tab? — Notes and Settings become URL-addressable with lazy route elements; activeContext leaves the Yjs doc. (3) Shell: RootLayout renders only shell concerns plus a GlobalOverlayOutlet; the pill bar is contributed by the reader/TTS feature, not imported by the layout. (4) Settings: registry of self-contained SettingsPanel descriptors ({id, label, component, order, danger}); dumb dialog shell with lazy panel mount and per-panel error boundary; one useConfirm abstraction replaces 25 native confirm/alert sites. (5) Design system: ui/ may import only lib/utils + Radix, enforced by an ESLint boundary rule; CompassPill split into a dumb ui/Pill shell plus per-variant feature pills with a single variant dispatcher; Modal as the lone dialog primitive; queue-based stacked Toast mounted above the router gate. (6) State: useUIStore becomes the shell UI store (settings-open, obsolete-lock, overlay registry); ephemeral per-device prefs in localStorage-backed slices, never the CRDT. (7) Hygiene: single createLogger API with no-console/no-alert lint; dbService.eraseEverything() owns wipe semantics for both reset entry points; window verification hooks gated to DEV/E2E.

overview: The shape of src/ itself: 266 runtime modules (~46.5k LOC) in nominal layers types/ lib/ db/ store/ hooks/ components/ workers/, composed by main.tsx/App.tsx. targetDesign: Strict enforceable layer stack: L0 types/ (imports nothing) → L1 lib/ (pure logic/services, no store/db/window side effects) → L2 db/ (idb schema + DBService; lib imports limited to logger + idb-write-lock) → L3 store/ (Zustand+Yjs state; never imports engines/managers) → L4 src/app/ (NEW: bootstrap, migrations, controllers for tts/sync/drive, repositories, host adapters createZustandEngineContext/createWorkerEngineClient/replicationSpec, debug hooks) → L5 hooks/ → L6 components/+layouts/ (never import db/ directly). Entries: main.tsx → app/bootstrap; workers/* and sw.ts → lib only. Rules enforced by dependency-cruiser + eslint no-restricted-imports + consistent-type-imports + import/no-cycle, CI-blocking: no getState() outside store/+app/; types/ imports nothing; worker runtime closure must exclude zustand/yjs/store (asserted by build-time chunk test); no module-level side effects outside bootstrap; one canonical import path per module; path aliases @types/@lib/@db/@store/@app/@components. EngineContext port pattern generalized to SyncPorts/DrivePorts/GooglePorts with adapters in app/. Stores shrink to state + pure actions; app/ controllers own cross-domain workflows (TTS command routing, sync lifecycle, import→ingest→store). React.lazy on routes and first-use dynamic import of heavy services once side effects are gone. Migrations become explicit awaited boot steps with static imports and real error propagation.

subsystem: Cross-cutting: type safety, error handling, logging (type-safety-errors)
overview: The cross-cutting infrastructure deciding whether Versicle is hard to break: TS strictness (tsconfig.app.json, eslint.config.js), error taxonomy (src/types/errors.ts, 5 classes), error propagation (264 production catch blocks), runtime boundary validation (src/lib/sync/validators.ts zod schemas, src/db/validators.ts), and logging/observability (src/lib/logger.ts, src/lib/tts/TTSFlightRecorder.ts, useToastStore, components/ErrorBoundary.tsx). Census (production code, excluding tests): 138 'as any', 81 ': any', 11 'as unknown as', 13 @ts-expect-error, 245 eslint-disable comments (611 repo-wide for no-explicit-any), 112 console.* lines bypassing the logger, 27 empty + ~28 silent catch blocks. Headline: the scaffolding exists but is unenforced and disconnected — zod validators are dead code, the error taxonomy covers only the DB domain, 246 test files are never type-checked, and observability covers exactly one subsystem (TTS).

targetDesign: 1) Error taxonomy: single AppError base with stable code union (DB_*/SYNC_*/TTS_*/GENAI_*/DRIVE_*/INGEST_*), ES2022 cause, context, retryable, toJSON/fromJSON for worker crossings; vendor errors mapped to AppError at exactly one module per boundary (handleDbError pattern). 2) Conventions: services throw typed errors for unexpected failures; expected outcomes return discriminated Results; no empty catch without a reason comment; UI maps error.code via one presentError helper; global handlers become last-resort snapshot+toast, not the designed channel. 3) Boundary validation map (zod at every ingress): backup import (schema + dry-run Y.applyUpdate + auto pre-restore checkpoint before destruction), Firestore (WorkspaceMetadata + post-merge Yjs entity validation with quarantine, observe-then-enforce rollout), GenAI (zod-first, Gemini responseSchema derived, semantic clamps), network JSON (Drive/GoogleTTS/Piper/dictionary), localStorage via typed readLocal helper. 4) Observability: generalize TTSFlightRecorder into a namespaced FlightRecorder (TTS/SYNC/DB/GENAI/INGEST/UI); createLogger mirrors warn/error into it; ErrorBoundary and unhandledrejection snapshot on crash; single Export Diagnostics UI; delete legacy Logger; no-console outside logger.ts. 5) Type-debt eradication: complete epubjs.d.ts + one typed ReaderEngine adapter as sole epubjs importer; BookViewModel for selectors; tsconfig.test.json so all 246 test files compile; staged compiler flags (noUncheckedIndexedAccess first); ESLint recommendedTypeChecked + no-floating-promises + no-explicit-any:error with a CI ratchet driving production as-any (138) and disable comments (245) to ~0.

overview: Cross-cutting audit of keyboard, screen-reader, focus, language, and motion behavior. Key surfaces: two competing global keydown registries (src/hooks/useReaderNavigation.ts, src/components/reader/ReaderTTSController.tsx) both mounted by ReaderView; the epub.js iframe reading surface and its six overlays (src/hooks/useEpubReader.ts, PinyinOverlay, AnnotationMarkerOverlay); TTS playback UI (CompassPill, UnifiedAudioPanel, TTSQueue); GlobalSettingsDialog fake tabs; the shadcn/Radix primitive layer (src/components/ui/*); theming/motion in src/index.css, dead tailwind.config.js, and hardcoded lang="en" in index.html. Verdict: per-widget ARIA hygiene is surprisingly decent (Radix + aria-label discipline, clearly from a one-off agent pass), but there is zero app-level a11y architecture — no shortcut owner, no announcer, no focus policy, no language attribution, no motion policy — and zero tooling (no axe, no jsx-a11y lint) to stop regression.

debts.title: Two overlapping global keyboard registries: double page turns, destructive Space/Escape conflicts
debts.fix: Single KeyboardShortcutService at the app shell: one window listener + one rendition bridge, declarative scoped registration (global<reader<tts-active<overlay), dev-mode collision errors, generated shortcut-help overlay. Delete both ad-hoc listeners. Interim hotfix: gate useReaderNavigation arrows on TTS status and bridge the controller to the rendition.

debts.title: No aria-live channel for TTS playback state — the core interaction is silent to AT
debts.fix: LiveAnnouncer mounted once in RootLayout (persistent polite+assertive regions, useAnnounce() hook + store-subscribable announce()). Wire TTS state transitions (debounced, chapter granularity), toast pipeline, and long-op status through it.

debts.title: Hardcoded lang="en" with zero language attribution for Chinese content
debts.fix: Set documentElement.lang at boot; require a lang prop on every component rendering book-derived text, fed from BookMetadata.language; wrap hanzi spans in lang="zh" in mixed content. Enforce via rewrite checklist/lint.

debts.title: Focusable buttons inside aria-hidden overlay; nested interactive elements
debts.fix: Drop aria-hidden from marker wrapper (keep pointer-events strategy) + roving tabindex; VocabTile becomes a single button with aria-pressed and focus-triggered definition popover; BookCard uses a stretched real button/link with the menu as a sibling.

debts.title: epub.js iframe reading surface has no screen-reader contract
debts.fix: In the ReaderShell workstream: name the iframe (title="Book content: {title}") where the sandbox patch runs; un-suppress contextmenu (or scope to Android); define and axe-verify the SR reading model for the same-origin iframe; require every overlay to declare decorative (aria-hidden) vs interactive via a shared ReaderOverlay wrapper.

debts.title: Zero automated a11y verification (no axe, no jsx-a11y lint); concern exists as two hand-rolled test files
debts.fix: Three layers before component rewrites: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y in eslint.config; vitest-axe smoke in the shared component test harness; @axe-core/playwright CI scans of the five core surfaces (library, reader+TTS, audio deck, settings, annotation pill) failing on serious/critical.

debts.title: Three competing tab implementations; settings/audio tabs invisible to AT
debts.fix: Mandate the Radix Tabs primitive for all tabbed UIs; settings registry renders nav via Tabs orientation="vertical"; delete the LexiconManager hand-rolled variant.

debts.title: No focus management policy: sidebars, pill remount-morphing, immersive mode, route changes
debts.fix: Sidebars become Radix Sheet/non-modal dialogs (focus enter/restore/Escape free); pill variants morph via internal state without remount and manage focus on mode entry; add landmarks + skip link in RootLayout; per-route document.title + focus-to-main on navigation.

debts.title: Motion layer unowned: dead Tailwind config, uninstalled animation plugin, no prefers-reduced-motion
debts.fix: Install tw-animate-css or delete the dead classes; remove App.css and tailwind.config.js (port tokens to @theme); add a global prefers-reduced-motion override plus a useReducedMotion() helper for JS-driven scroll/animation.

debts.title: Toast live region mounts with its content, hover-only pause, single-slot store
debts.fix: Rewritten Toast primitive: persistent live-region container at root with content injected, toast queue, timer pause on hover and focus-within, longer default duration for alerts; share the pipe with LiveAnnouncer.

debts.title: No aria-current/list semantics on stateful lists (TTS queue, TOC)
debts.fix: aria-current="true" on active queue item and TOC chapter; ol/role=list wrapper for the queue; device-marker info into accessible names.

debts.title: Contrast gaps: unvalidated custom themes, 10px text, fixed highlight colors
debts.fix: Contrast validation in the theme picker; 12px minimum for informational text in the new Pill; luminance-derived highlight opacity/blend; forced-colors audit in verification.

couplings: TTS subsystem owns reader keyboard policy: ReaderTTSController.tsx:162-207 (TTS UI) and useReaderNavigation.ts:73-120 (reader) bind window keys against the same ReaderView callbacks without knowing about each other (ReaderView.tsx:1078,1117).

CompassPill chapter-nav buttons dispatch an untyped window CustomEvent 'reader:chapter-nav' (CompassPill.tsx:317-322) consumed by ReaderView.tsx:896-915, which re-routes to TTS section skip vs page turn — the pill's accessible actions depend on a global event contract.

Reading-surface a11y depends on epub.js internals: sandbox MutationObserver patching (useEpubReader.ts:350-379) and manual SVG-annotation DOM sweeps (ReaderTTSController.tsx:58-73) via (rendition as any).
Duplicate pill hosts: ReaderControlBar.tsx (mounted in RootLayout) and AudioReaderHUD.tsx (no non-test importer found — apparently dead) both implement CompassPill variant arbitration.
The single-slot useToastStore/ToastContainer is the de-facto announcement channel for TTS, sync, and import errors — all inherit its live-region unreliability.
keepers: The Radix/shadcn primitive layer (Modal, Sheet, Popover, Select, DropdownMenu, Switch, Checkbox, Tabs, Slider, Checkbox) with consistent focus-visible ring styling; Slider.tsx:14-36 explicitly forwards aria-label/labelledby/valuetext to the thumb.
Icon-button aria-label discipline across the app (ReaderView header 1146-1267, SearchPanel, BookCard menu trigger, PasswordInput) and BookCover alt text (BookCover.tsx:78).

CompassPill's accessible-name composition: formatTimeAccessible (CompassPill.tsx:342-349), aria-pressed state, keyboard activation handlers — plus its dedicated a11y test file (the pattern is right even where structure needs fixing).
VisualSettings.tsx as the model settings panel: labeled sliders with role=status aria-live value readouts, htmlFor'd switches, Radix Tabs.
PinyinOverlay's deliberate aria-hidden + pointer-events-none for a decorative geometry overlay — the precedent for the overlay contract.
GlobalSettingsDialog's VisuallyHidden ModalTitle/Description (lines 463-470) and the sr-only aria-live loading spans across library/settings.
Viewport meta without user-scalable=no and rem-based font-size normalization in the reader (useEpubReader.ts:52-90) — zoom and text scaling work.
targetDesign: Accessibility as four shared services plus contracts baked into the new primitive APIs, enforced by tooling. (1) KeyboardShortcutService: the only window/rendition keydown listener; declarative scoped registration (global→reader→tts-active→overlay), when-predicates, dev-mode collision errors, generated shortcut-help overlay; raw keydown listeners lint-banned. (2) LiveAnnouncer in RootLayout: persistent polite+assertive regions, useAnnounce() + store-subscribable announce(); wired to TTS state transitions (debounced, chapter granularity), the queued Toast pipeline, long-op status, and route changes. (3) Language correctness: documentElement.lang at boot; lang prop required on every book-text-rendering primitive, fed from BookMetadata.language. (4) Motion policy: global prefers-reduced-motion override + useReducedMotion() for JS scroll/animation; dead tailwind.config.js and App.css removed; animation utilities either backed by an installed plugin or deleted. Component contracts for the overhaul workstreams: every reader overlay declares decorative vs interactive via a ReaderOverlay wrapper; all tabbed UIs use Radix Tabs; no nested interactives; aria-current on active list items; pill variants morph without remount and manage focus; iframe titled; landmarks + skip link; per-route document.title. Verification pyramid: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y → vitest-axe in the shared component harness → @axe-core/playwright CI scans of five core surfaces, plus a manual VoiceOver/TalkBack smoke script; every component-rewrite workstream gets an explicit a11y acceptance section referencing these gates.

overview: This subsystem does not exist — the report documents the gap. Versicle has zero i18n infrastructure: no i18n library in package.json, navigator.language never read, no locale preference in any store, index.html:2 fixed to lang="en", no lang= attribute anywhere in JSX, and ~800+ hardcoded English strings (348 quoted phrases + 186 JSX text nodes across 47+ files, 159 aria-labels, 81 showToast sites, 24 confirm/alert sites). The only locale-aware code is the (sound) content-language pipeline: book.language from EPUB metadata via src/lib/language-utils.ts, threaded into segmentation, fonts, lexicon, and TTS. Sixteen toLocale* call sites format with the system locale with no policy; Intl.RelativeTimeFormat/NumberFormat/Collator are used zero times. The product explicitly targets Mandarin learners, making a Chinese UI locale the obvious eventual ask — and the overhaul is about to rewrite exactly the seams where externalization is cheap.

Broad aria-label coverage (159 sites) — accessibility strings exist and are well-placed; externalize in place, don't re-author
normalizeLanguageCode and the GenAI layer's existing book-language awareness (GenAIService.ts:232)
targetDesign: Decision (proposed): "i18n-ready, en-only at launch" — adopt a message-catalog layer during the already-planned rewrites; ship English only; zh-Hans/zh-Hant later touches catalog files only. If declared a non-goal instead, record the ADR and still do the formatting/error/lang-attribute correctness fixes. Library constraints from this codebase: works in plain TS and the TTS Web Worker (not React-context-bound), type-safe keys, ICU plurals, small lazy-loaded runtime — paraglide-js (preferred) or @lingui both qualify; react-i18next does not without bolt-ons. Architecture: src/lib/locale/ owns getUILocale() (per-device localStorage pref → navigator.language → 'en'; NOT in the Yjs doc — needed pre-boot and devices differ), documentElement.lang sync, and cached Intl formatters (date, relative-time, bytes, percent, duration, collator) replacing the 16 ad-hoc sites and all duplicates. Catalog: domain-namespaced keys with an errors.* namespace keyed 1:1 by error code so presentError(code, params) is a catalog lookup; TTS spoken strings resolved by book.language, not UI locale. Choke-point contracts (toast queue, useConfirm, settings-registry labels, presentError) take typed message keys + params, never prose. Two-locale rule documented in architecture.md: UI locale governs chrome/formatting/collation; book.language governs segmentation/voices/pinyin/spoken filler/content lang attributes. Lint: no-literal-string enabled per directory as migrated; no-alert once useConfirm lands.

subsystem: Third-party licensing, provenance & notice inventory for vendored/forked assets (gap-third-party-licensing-provenan)
overview: The legal/provenance layer underlying every Versicle distribution channel (web dist/, PWA precache, Android APK). Key files: LICENSE (GPL-3.0, the only license file in the repo), package.json:5,67-73 (GPL declaration + three forked git deps), public/fonts/* (modified OFL fonts), public/dict/cedict.json (CC BY-SA 4.0, 14MB), public/piper/* (18.8MB gitignored-but-shipped blobs embedding GPL-3 espeak-ng), patches/ (MPL-2.0 patch), src/data/bible-lexicon.ts, scripts/compile-dict.cjs + patch_piper_worker.js (provenance-bearing build tooling), README.md:185-197 and GeneralSettingsTab.tsx:100-202 / VisualSettings.tsx:144-171 (attribution surfaces). Headline: zero license incompatibilities exist — GPL-3.0-or-later is compatible with everything found (incl. AGPL ua-parser-js and GPL media-session) — but notice retention, provenance recording, and one genuine OFL violation are unaddressed, and the planned vendoring of forks + piper artifacts will crystallize the gaps.

keepers: README.md:185-197 Licenses & Attributions — accurate CC BY-SA 4.0 ShareAlike statement and honest disclosure of font modifications; correct the RFN consequence, keep the prose
