targetDesign: One checked-in source of truth, everything else generated. (1) third-party/inventory.json: every redistributed artifact — npm bundle deps (auto-extracted from package-lock), vendored packages, public/ data assets (fonts, dict, piper blobs, EPUBs, Drive logo, audio), runtime-fetched payloads — with name, version, upstream URL, SPDX license, license-text path, provenance, local modifications, distribution channels. (2) Generated at build time: dist/THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt (compensates for stripped banners), the in-app <Credits/> data (replacing both hand-written JSX blocks), and a repo-root THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md. (3) CI license gate: allowlist check over package-lock plus staleness check on generated notices; fails on UNKNOWN or new copyleft without an inventory entry. (4) Per-vendored-directory provenance: packages/*/LICENSE retained verbatim with fork headers; public/piper/PROVENANCE.md (espeak-ng GPL-3, piper-phonemize, build recipe, local patch list); public/fonts/OFL.txt; public/dict/cedict.meta.json. (5) Fixed artifacts: fonts renamed off the OFL Reserved Font Names with the fonttools script committed; dict compiler stamps metadata and fails hard instead of writing a mock; patches/README.md; ua-parser-js replaced or consciously recorded. (6) LICENSING.md documenting that the combined work is GPL-3.0-or-later and why (GPL/AGPL inputs enumerated), preventing accidental relicensing. Sequencing: land items 1-3 before the platform-build fork-vendoring and tts-providers piper-vendoring PRs so vendoring is gated; the font rename is the only user-visible change and needs a persisted-preference string migration.

subsystem: Privacy posture: data-egress map and network boundary architecture
overview: Audit of every network egress path against the README claim "We don't know what you read. No analytics" (README.md:5,10). The "no analytics" half holds: zero telemetry SDKs. The rest is architecturally unenforced. Egress surface: 11 fetch() sites across 8 files (GenAIService via @google/generative-ai SDK, GoogleTTS/OpenAI/LemonFox providers, PiperProvider + piper-utils → huggingface.co, DriveService, plus same-origin-only sites), an unbundled XHR loader in public/piper/piper_worker.js that imports remote onnxruntime code from cdnjs, the firebase SDK (user-owned project), and @capgo OAuth. Key trigger logic lives in AudioContentPipeline.ts / TableAdaptationProcessor.ts (auto-fired Gemini analyses during TTS playback and next-chapter prefetch), gated only by global flags at AudioContentPipeline.ts:473. CSP (nginx.conf:12, vite.config.ts:35) has wildcard https: in connect-src; the Android build has no CSP at all.

debts.title: No network gateway / egress policy — fetch scattered, boundary unenforceable
debts.fix: src/lib/net/ with a typed destination registry (host, data classification, consent, timeout, offline behavior), a NetworkGateway.egress() all call sites route through, an ESLint ban on raw fetch/XHR outside src/lib/net/, CSP generated from the registry, and a registry==CSP unit test.

debts.fix: Redact payloads by default (counts+hashes); full payloads only in an in-memory ring buffer under debug mode; explicit partialize allowlist; persist-version migration strips existing logs.

BaseCloudProvider.getOrFetch (BaseCloudProvider.ts:74-116): cache-first + in-flight dedup + permanent TTS audio cache minimizes repeat egress by design
Prompt minimization in detectContentTypes (GenAIService.ts:282-291: 8-word/120-char truncation) and the zero-egress deterministic detection strategy (AudioContentPipeline.ts:466-471)
Capacitor hardening (capacitor.config.ts): androidScheme https, cleartext false, allowNavigation []
Genuinely no analytics/telemetry SDKs, no third-party scripts in index.html, self-hosted fonts, BYO-keys/BYO-Firebase no-first-party-server model
Sanitizer fundamentals (sanitizer.ts): script stripping, external-CSS-link removal, reverse-tabnabbing fix — extend, don't rewrite
targetDesign: A single egress boundary in src/lib/net/: (1) destinations.ts — typed registry of every allowed destination with hosts, data classification (book-content/book-derived/metadata/binary-asset/auth/remote-code), consent requirement, timeout, retry, and offline behavior; (2) NetworkGateway.egress(destinationId, request) enforcing consent (per-book bit for non-interactive book-content destinations), AbortController timeouts, offline fallbacks, and per-destination byte/char counters (replacing the dead CostEstimator); (3) ESLint ban on raw fetch/XHR outside src/lib/net plus a unit test asserting registry hosts == CSP hosts; (4) CSP generated from the registry into nginx, vite preview, and an index.html meta tag (covering Capacitor), with https: removed from connect-src and img-src. Piper becomes genuinely local: onnxruntime vendored into /public/piper, voices.json cached with offline fallback, voice metadata persisted at download time. GenAI logging is redacted by default (counts/hashes persisted; full payloads in-memory only under debug). The sanitizer blocks remote EPUB resources by default. Provider contracts gain a locality field surfaced in the voice picker. architecture.md gains a generated Privacy & data flow matrix and settings gains a Network Activity panel rendered from the same registry — making the README claim continuously auditable rather than aspirational.
